refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (local vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (teleoperation, safety services, vehicle control) and anchors verification to measurable, time-bound operational data.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5-day week). Log task completion (volume moved, tonnage), elapsed time, and safety incidents. Compare achieved productivity against productivity_target. Validate that all safety plan requirements were met during trial.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and accuracy.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote operator to command and monitor vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient for safe, productive task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and prevent unsafe task sequences.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The solution shall provide real-time telemetry (position, load, cycle status) to enable productivity measurement and operator situational awareness.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate autonomous vehicle with remote teleoperation fallback; enforce safety rules via onboard logic; stream telemetry to cloud dashboard for productivity analytics.
DP-B: Deploy vehicle with on-site operator control only; implement mechanical/hydraulic safety interlocks; manual time-and-motion logging for productivity measurement.
DP-C: Hybrid architecture: vehicle with dual control modes (local + remote); safety services as independent arbiter; telemetry-driven productivity tracking with real-time alerts.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Hybrid architecture with dual control modes (local operator or tele-operator), independent safety services arbiter enforcing worksite safety plan, and continuous telemetry-driven productivity tracking with real-time performance alerts and compliance monitoring.

potential_sources: Safety services must couple to vehicle control (command veto/override) – acceptable; safety is a cross-cutting concern.
Telemetry must couple to both vehicle and teleoperation – acceptable; telemetry is a data backbone.
Operator interface (local vs. remote) couples to vehicle control logic – acceptable; both modes share same vehicle state machine.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C exhibits controlled coupling: safety arbiter is a logical hub (not a bottleneck), telemetry is a broadcast mechanism (not a single point of failure), and dual control modes share vehicle dynamics (no duplication). Coupling is justified by safety criticality and productivity measurement requirements.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Network latency for teleoperation is ≤ 200 ms round-trip; if exceeded, remote operator safety and productivity degrade. Risk: Mitigation via local autonomy fallback and safety interlocks.

Assumption: Worksite safety plan is formalized and machine-readable (or can be encoded as rules). Risk: If plan is vague or changes frequently, safety services logic becomes brittle. Mitigation: Establish safety plan versioning and validation process.

Assumption: Productivity target is achievable with hybrid control (not requiring full autonomy). Risk: If target demands autonomous optimization beyond operator capability, DP-C may be insufficient. Mitigation: Validate target against baseline equipment performance.

Assumption: Telemetry infrastructure (sensors, network, storage) is reliable and available. Risk: Data loss or latency spikes prevent real-time productivity tracking. Mitigation: Implement local buffering and graceful degradation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-C is sound. Recommend explicit definition of: (1) safety rule set and arbiter logic (e.g., geofence, load limits, operator certification checks), (2) telemetry schema and update frequency, (3) fallback behavior when teleoperation link is lost, (4) productivity metric calculation (e.g., volume per cycle, cycles per hour, utilization factor).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration boundary encompassing vehicle control, teleoperation, safety services, and telemetry aggregation. Owns the FR-to-DP mapping and coordinates all sub-system interactions.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time vehicle state (position, load, cycle status, safety flags) streamed to Site Manager dashboard and Tele-operator console for productivity tracking and situational awareness.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Task commands from on-site Operator or Tele-operator (dig, load, transport, dump) and worksite safety plan updates from Safety Manager.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety violations, geofence breaches, load limit exceedances, and operator certification failures broadcast to Safety Manager and Tele-operator.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication channel (4G/5G or private network) connecting vehicle, teleoperation console, and cloud services for command, telemetry, and safety alert exchange.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integration boundary. All sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are logically contained. Minimal diffusion: only external stakeholder interfaces (Site Manager, Operator, Safety Manager) cross the boundary. This is the correct top-level IM assignment.

Vehicle module owns earthmoving task execution and local control logic. However, productivity measurement, safety arbitration, and teleoperation command routing are not vehicle-internal; they diffuse across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Vehicle is a sub-component, not the top-level IM.

Teleoperation module owns remote operator interface and command transmission. Productivity measurement and safety enforcement are not teleoperation-specific; they require integration with Vehicle and Safety Services. Teleoperation is a sub-component, not the top-level IM.

Safety Services owns safety rule enforcement and arbiter logic. Productivity measurement, task execution, and teleoperation are not safety-specific; they require integration with Vehicle and Teleoperation. Safety Services is a sub-component, not the top-level IM.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: CR-NGHE-001 is a system-level KPI that requires coordinated action across vehicle control, teleoperation, safety enforcement, and telemetry. No single sub-component (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) can independently satisfy the requirement. The NGHE Solution boundary is the correct IM assignment because it owns the integration logic, interface orchestration, and end-to-end productivity measurement.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution IM is too coarse; it may mask architectural decisions about sub-component boundaries and interfaces. Mitigation: Decompose into sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) and assign each to appropriate sub-module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) in next decomposition level.

Risk: Productivity measurement logic may be diffused across Vehicle (cycle timing), Teleoperation (operator efficiency), and Safety Services (constraint violations). Mitigation: Establish a dedicated telemetry aggregation and analytics function within NGHE Solution to centralize productivity KPI calculation.

Risk: Safety arbiter (Safety Services) and vehicle control (Vehicle) may have conflicting command priorities. Mitigation: Define explicit command arbitration protocol (e.g., safety veto overrides operator command) and encode in NGHE Solution integration logic.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: NGHE Solution is the correct top-level IM. Recommend: (1) Establish explicit sub-module boundaries for Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry Aggregation in next decomposition level. (2) Define interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) with schema, latency, and reliability requirements. (3) Assign ownership of each sub-module to a specific team (e.g., Vehicle team, Teleoperation team, Safety team, Integration team).

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid dual-control architecture with independent safety arbiter and telemetry-driven productivity tracking) directly addresses FR-NGHE-001 (achieve productivity target while maintaining safety compliance). The DP is solution-agnostic (does not prescribe specific hardware or software), logically sound (separates concerns: control, safety, measurement), and feasible (hybrid control is proven in heavy equipment industry). No independence axiom violation detected.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: Productivity measurement logic may be scattered across Vehicle (cycle timing), Teleoperation (operator efficiency), and Safety Services (constraint violations). Mitigation: Centralize productivity KPI calculation in a dedicated telemetry aggregation module within NGHE Solution.

Diffusion Risk 2: Safety arbiter (Safety Services) and vehicle control (Vehicle) may have conflicting command priorities. Mitigation: Define explicit command arbitration protocol and encode in NGHE Solution integration logic.
Diffusion Risk 3: Telemetry schema and update frequency may vary across sub-modules. Mitigation: Establish standardized telemetry interface contract in NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution IM correctly contains the DP logic. Sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) will be assigned in next decomposition level. Current IM assignment is appropriate for system-level integration and does not prematurely constrain sub-component design.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via representative operations trial: (1) Instrument vehicle with telemetry sensors (position, load, cycle status). (2) Log operator commands and safety events. (3) Measure achieved productivity (m³/h or ton/h) over agreed period. (4) Validate safety plan compliance. (5) Compare against productivity_target. Verification is measurable, repeatable, and time-bound.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-NGHE-001 is allocated to NGHE Solution IM with clear sub-FR decomposition (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) for next level. Each sub-FR will be allocated to appropriate sub-module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1: Formalize productivity_target value and measurement methodology (e.g., m³/h, ton/h, utilization factor, cycle time). Current FR uses placeholder 'productivity_target'; actual value must be defined by customer and encoded in acceptance criteria.

Revision 2: Formalize worksite safety plan as machine-readable rules or constraints (e.g., geofence, load limits, operator certification). Current FR references 'worksite safety plan and operational policies' without specifics; safety services cannot enforce vague requirements.

Revision 3: Define fallback behavior when teleoperation link is lost (e.g., vehicle returns to safe state, operator switches to local control, system halts). Current DP assumes network availability; explicit fallback logic is needed.

Revision 4: Establish telemetry schema, update frequency, and reliability requirements (e.g., 100 ms update, 99.9% availability). Current DP assumes 'continuous telemetry'; specifics are needed for implementation.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level system KPI that requires decomposition into sub-FRs (task execution, teleoperation, safety enforcement, telemetry) and assignment to sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). The current level (NGHE Solution) is correct for system integration, but further refinement is needed to allocate responsibilities to sub-components and validate that each sub-FR can be independently satisfied. Zigzag down to next decomposition level to refine sub-FRs and assign to sub-modules.
decision.justification_detail: The refined FR and selected DP are sound and pass gate review. However, the IM assignment (NGHE Solution) is a system-level integration boundary that masks architectural decisions about sub-component design. To validate that DP-C is realizable and that each sub-component can be independently owned and tested, we must decompose FR-NGHE-001 into sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) and assign each to appropriate sub-module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). This zigzag ensures that the system-level DP is grounded in feasible sub-component designs.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and accuracy sufficient to achieve productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote operator to command and monitor vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient for safe, productive task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and prevent unsafe task sequences.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The solution shall provide real-time telemetry (position, load, cycle status) to enable productivity measurement and operator situational awareness.

assumptions: Assumption 1: Productivity_target is achievable with hybrid control (local + remote) and does not require full autonomy.
Assumption 2: Network latency for teleoperation is ≤ 200 ms round-trip; if exceeded, remote operator safety and productivity degrade.
Assumption 3: Worksite safety plan is formalized and machine-readable (or can be encoded as rules).
Assumption 4: Telemetry infrastructure (sensors, network, storage) is reliable and available.
Assumption 5: Site Manager, Equipment Operator, Tele-operator, and Safety Manager are available and trained to use NGHE solution.
Assumption 6: Representative operations trial is feasible and representative of actual worksite conditions.
open_risks: Risk 1: Productivity_target may be unachievable with hybrid control; full autonomy may be required. Mitigation: Validate target against baseline equipment performance and operator efficiency data.
Risk 2: Network latency may exceed 200 ms due to worksite conditions (remote location, poor coverage). Mitigation: Implement local autonomy fallback and safety interlocks.
Risk 3: Worksite safety plan may be vague or change frequently, making safety services logic brittle. Mitigation: Establish safety plan versioning and validation process.
Risk 4: Telemetry infrastructure may be unreliable or unavailable, preventing real-time productivity tracking. Mitigation: Implement local buffering and graceful degradation.

Risk 5: Sub-component designs (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) may be incompatible or have conflicting requirements. Mitigation: Establish interface contracts and integration testing in next decomposition level.
Risk 6: Safety criticality (high) may require additional validation, certification, or regulatory compliance. Mitigation: Engage Safety Manager and Maintenance/Quality early in sub-component design.
evidence_needed: Evidence 1: Baseline productivity data for comparable equipment (m³/h or ton/h) to validate productivity_target feasibility.
Evidence 2: Network latency measurements from representative worksites to validate teleoperation feasibility.
Evidence 3: Formalized worksite safety plan (rules, constraints, geofences, load limits) to enable safety services design.
Evidence 4: Telemetry sensor specifications and reliability data to validate measurement feasibility.
Evidence 5: Operator efficiency data (cycle time, task completion rate) to validate hybrid control feasibility.
Evidence 6: Safety certification and regulatory requirements (e.g., ISO, ANSI, local regulations) to inform safety services design.
Evidence 7: Representative operations trial plan and acceptance criteria to validate end-to-end system performance.
